setup_task.sh specifies this path as output location

Literature review: male mean 172.7±7.7mm (range ~165-180mm), female mean 168.4±9.3mm (range ~159-177mm), with observed populations showing lower extreme around 150mm. Consistent with verifier.py line 35-36 and export_result.sh validation logic.

Conservative upper bound exceeding typical human range (~190mm max observed). Dataset DICOM FOV of 245mm axial plane allows measurement up to this value. Used in verifier.py line 35-36 for plausibility checking.

Literature review: male mean 133.5±7.1mm (range ~126-141mm), female mean 130.1±8.3mm (range ~122-138mm), Thai populations 138-142mm. Lower extreme around 120mm reasonable. Consistent with verifier.py line 37-38.

Conservative upper bound exceeding typical human range (~150mm max observed in modern populations). Dataset DICOM FOV of 245mm axial plane allows measurement up to this value. Used in verifier.py line 37-38 for plausibility checking.
Task requires exactly 2 measurements: Maximum Cranial Length and Maximum Cranial Breadth. Verifier.py line 63-65 requires at least 2 numeric values in report.

privileged_info_summary: The CT Cranium dataset is the official InVesalius sample (patient ID 4055947867, case name 'CT 0051 - InVesalius Sample') containing 108 DICOM slices with 2mm thickness and 0.48mm pixel spacing, providing 245mm field of view. The measurement ranges (length: 150-210mm, breadth: 120-175mm) are validated against anthropological literature showing typical human cranial length 150-190mm and breadth 120-150mm. The 2-measurement requirement aligns with the task description requiring Maximum Cranial Length and Maximum Cranial Breadth.
